programmes.show_description: The junior doctors are now in their second week at the Royal Berkshire Hospital. Adrian does a tricky blood test on a critically-ill patient, and Tej spots some worrying test results.
It's the night shift for the junior doctors. Adrian's ego takes a hit as he bites off more than he can chew, and Ese must solve the mystery of a patient's unexplained collapse.
Stormzy: Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home to discuss his childhood, the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being a musician and role model.
A Tall Tail: Cat-breeding pro-wrestler Teddy Hart teams up with filmmaker Frederick Kroetsch to make a reality show, but things take a dark turn when two women make disturbing allegations.
Shopping from Home.
Queen Bee, Winter and Elliot: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Telford: In Shropshire, three schoolchildren, the DIY SOS team and hundreds of volunteers help 12-year-old Matthew return home from hospital for the first time in three years.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
Arundel: Nick Knowles meets a West Sussex wife and mother-of-four who was paralysed in a bicycle accident in the summer of 2015, and needs her home adapting for her wheelchair.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
New. Ollie, Star & Zak: Great Dane Star is rushed in after losing the use of her back legs. She's in a lot of pain and is sedated so Noel can determine the cause of her sudden paralysis.
New. Barney, Miranda & Billy: Tiny Yorkshire terrier Barney is just ten months old, but he has a deformity in his neck which is compressing his spinal cord, leaving him unable even to stand.
Telford: In Shropshire, three schoolchildren, the DIY SOS team and hundreds of volunteers help 12-year-old Matthew return home from hospital for the first time in three years.
The Million Pound Build for Children in Need: The team head to Swansea to build a centre and supported housing for young people in care and leaving care for the Roots Foundation Wales.
In this 100th episode, the crews go cross country after a teenager's bike crash, attend a man who's had a nasty fall and help a woman with end-stage cancer.
Paramedic Kim climbs into a crushed car balanced over a fast-flowing river, while services across the Pennines are paralysed when a man plunges from a bridge onto a busy rail line.
A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.
The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a trip to the church to hear the banns being read, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret.
The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding.
The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Stacey's brother, while Nessa debates whether to share her big secret.
Dame Judi Dench: Louis meets the beloved Oscar-winning actress for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, and life away from acting.
The Nine Lives of Teddy Hart: Teddy meets aspiring wrestler Samantha Fiddler and takes her to Florida to train, but when he returns to Canada, Samantha's family report her missing.
Shopping from Home.
Righteous Kill: Part 2: Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off by his stepmother.
Bail Me Out: A drugs runner wearing a hoodie evades arrest, but crashes his stolen car, seriously injuring his passenger and leaving CID with three suspects.
Matters of the Mind: The resident magician of a social club turns to crime to fund a gambling habit, and falls prey to a blackmailer threatening to harm his girlfriend.
Leap of Faith: Part 1: When Nate pulls a suicidal woman from the Thames, Terry discovers she is carrying a bag of heroin. Beth tells Tony she has applied for a transfer.
Leap of Faith: Part 2: Beth plans to kiss Nate at her leaving party and finds herself working with him on a domestic incident involving a boxing club owner.
Decision Time: The driver of a lorry hijacked by masked raiders in an isolated lay-by admits to smuggling a woman and her child into the country to escape a violent marriage.
On the Money: Stolen goods are returned to the house they were taken from - apparently by a remorseful burglar - but as the case takes a twist, Leon's integrity is called into question.
The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground.
A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.
A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two YouTube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree.
Paramedic Kim climbs into a crushed car balanced over a fast-flowing river, while services across the Pennines are paralysed when a man plunges from a bridge onto a busy rail line.
A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.
The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.
St John Critical Care Paramedics from the Northern Emergency Services Trust find a motocross rider near death in a remote forest.
A man is at risk of losing his leg after a horrific accident involving a boat propeller, while another needs to be airlifted to hospital after a mysterious accident involving a tractor.
The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a trip to the church to hear the banns being read, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret.
The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding.
The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Stacey's brother, while Nessa debates whether to share her big secret.
